reasoning: C is the only response that grounds the supply side in the actual BLS OEWS metro figure (~150 musical-instrument repairers/tuners), correctly notes OEWS excludes self-employed, and applies both a piano-specific haircut and a self-employed multiplier; it also cites concrete CEO-education studies with real percentages (engineering 19.2%, economics 11.8%, S&P 28.1%), making it the most empirically disciplined and decision-ready. A and D are very close: both decompose cleanly, reconcile a Fermi build with an outside view, and best surface the representativeness/substitution and non-exhaustive-bucket traps on part (b); A edges D on bias-awareness narrative while D edges slightly on the city-vs-metro framing. B is weakest: a 1.3 tunings/piano/year blend and a national-occupation-to-metro extrapolation that fails to haircut for piano-specific share inflate it to ~250-310, and it under-handles the denominator that the others correctly separate.
